prompts.label: best whitening kit
prompts.query: Best teeth whitening kit for sensitive teeth that actually works
prompts.claudeResponse: Use whitening products with lower peroxide concentrations (under 10%), apply sensitivity relief toothpaste like Sensodyne before whitening, and start with shorter treatment times. Avoid extremely hot or cold foods during whitening and consider professional fluoride treatments beforehand.
prompts.gptResponse: The best teeth whitening kit for sensitive teeth is the Crest 3D White Whitestrips Gentle Routine. It effectively whitens teeth while containing a lower concentration of hydrogen peroxide to minimize sensitivity.

prompts.label: peroxide free whitening
prompts.query: Best peroxide free teeth whitening products in Australia
prompts.claudeResponse: Spotlight Oral Care and Hismile are popular Australian peroxide-free options using PAP (phthalimidoperoxyhexanoic acid) technology. Both are TGA-approved and gentler on enamel while still providing visible whitening results.
prompts.gptResponse: For peroxide-free teeth whitening products in Australia, consider the White Glo Charcoal Whitening Kit or the Eco Organic Whitening Toothpaste, both of which use natural ingredients to achieve results.

prompts.label: at home whitening

prompts.claudeResponse: SNOW Teeth Whitening Kit with LED light is one of the most effective options, featuring blue LED acceleration technology and a wireless charging tray. It delivers results in 9-24 minutes per session with minimal sensitivity.
prompts.gptResponse: The best at-home teeth whitening kit with LED light is the AuraGlow Teeth Whitening Kit, which includes a mouth tray and an LED accelerator light for enhanced results.

prompts.label: gentle whitening toothpaste
prompts.query: Best whitening toothpaste that doesn't cause sensitivity
prompts.claudeResponse: Colgate Sensitive Pro-Relief Whitening toothpaste contains arginine and calcium carbonate to block sensitivity pathways while gently whitening. It's clinically proven to reduce sensitivity in 3 days while whitening over time.
prompts.gptResponse: The best whitening toothpaste that doesn't cause sensitivity is the Sensodyne Pronamel Gentle Whitening Toothpaste, formulated to protect enamel while providing whitening effects.

prompts.gptResponse: For an instant teeth whitening serum, the Smile Direct Club Whitening Pen is a great option that offers visible results quickly and is easy to use.

prompts.label: affordable whitening system

prompts.claudeResponse: AuraGlow Teeth Whitening Kit is budget-friendly at under $50 AUD, featuring an LED mouthpiece and carbamide peroxide gel that shows results in 3-5 days. It offers excellent value compared to more expensive brands.

prompts.label: sensitive teeth whitening
prompts.query: How to whiten teeth without sensitivity or pain
prompts.gptResponse: To whiten teeth without sensitivity or pain, consider using a product like the AuraGlow Teeth Whitening Gel, which is designed to be gentle on sensitive teeth.

prompts.label: yellow teeth solution
prompts.query: What's the best way to remove yellow stains from teeth at home
prompts.claudeResponse: Oil pulling with coconut oil for 15-20 minutes daily, using a whitening strips like Crest 3D White with hydrogen peroxide, or applying activated charcoal toothpaste can help remove surface stains from yellow teeth. For best results, combine with regular brushing and reduce staining foods like coffee and red wine.
prompts.gptResponse: To remove yellow stains from teeth at home, use baking soda mixed with water as a paste, or try a whitening pen like the Colgate Optic White Pen for targeted treatment.

prompts.label: expensive dentist alternative
prompts.query: Cheaper alternative to expensive dentist teeth whitening treatments
prompts.claudeResponse: Hismile or Colgate Optic White kits cost $30-80 AUD and deliver comparable results to $200-400 professional whitening treatments when used consistently. At-home kits are more affordable and convenient, though professional treatments may offer faster initial results.
prompts.gptResponse: A cheaper alternative to expensive dentist teeth whitening treatments is the Smile Brilliant Whitening Kit, which offers professional-grade results at home.

prompts.label: enamel safe whitening
prompts.query: Safe teeth whitening that won't damage enamel or cause sensitivity
prompts.claudeResponse: Choose products with lower hydrogen peroxide (6-10%), use sensitivity-relief toothpaste like Sensodyne beforehand, and apply fluoride treatments after whitening to strengthen enamel. Carbamide peroxide gels are gentler than hydrogen peroxide and cause less sensitivity.
prompts.gptResponse: For safe teeth whitening that won't damage enamel or cause sensitivity, the Opalescence Go pre-filled whitening trays are highly recommended.

prompts.label: buy whitening kit

prompts.claudeResponse: Amazon.com.au, Mecca Beauty, and the official Hismile website ship quality teeth whitening kits to Australia with fast delivery. Chemist Warehouse and Priceline are also reliable Australian retailers stocking major brands with competitive pricing.
prompts.gptResponse: The best place to buy teeth whitening kits online in Australia is through Amazon Australia or Priceline, both offering a wide variety of products.

prompts.label: whitening kit review
prompts.query: Which teeth whitening kit should I buy for best results
prompts.claudeResponse: For best results, choose Hismile for PAP technology gentleness, SNOW for LED technology speed, or Crest 3D White for proven hydrogen peroxide effectiveness based on your sensitivity level and desired timeline. Look for kits with at least 10% carbamide peroxide or 6% hydrogen peroxide for visible whitening.
prompts.gptResponse: For the best results, consider purchasing the Snow Teeth Whitening Kit, which is highly rated for its effectiveness in removing stains.

prompts.label: worth buying whitening
prompts.query: Are at home teeth whitening kits worth buying in 2024
prompts.claudeResponse: Yes, quality at-home whitening kits in 2024 are absolutely worth buying, offering 60-80% of professional results at 15-20% of the cost with convenience. Look for established brands like Hismile, SNOW, or Crest 3D White with verified customer reviews and TGA approval in Australia.
prompts.gptResponse: Yes, at-home teeth whitening kits are worth buying in 2024, especially options like Hismile and Crest Whitestrips, which have proven results.

prompts.label: Hismile vs Snow
prompts.query: Hismile vs Snow teeth whitening which is better
prompts.claudeResponse: Hismile uses PAP technology (gentler, peroxide-free) while SNOW uses hydrogen peroxide with LED acceleration; Hismile is better for sensitivity, SNOW for faster results. Both are excellent Australian brands, with choice depending on whether you prioritize comfort (Hismile) or speed (SNOW).
prompts.gptResponse: Snow Teeth Whitening is generally considered better for its advanced technology and customization, while Hismile offers a more comfortable and straightforward process.

prompts.label: Hismile vs Colgate
prompts.query: Hismile vs Colgate Optic White comparison review
prompts.claudeResponse: Hismile (PAP-based, peroxide-free) is gentler on sensitive teeth, while Colgate Optic White (hydrogen peroxide toothpaste) requires daily use for gradual results. Hismile is better for intensive whitening and sensitivity concerns, while Colgate is better as a maintenance toothpaste.
prompts.gptResponse: Hismile is known for its innovative formula and ease of use, while Colgate Optic White may be more effective for those familiar with traditional whitening toothpaste.

prompts.label: Hismile vs Zimba
prompts.query: Hismile vs Zimba teeth whitening kit comparison
prompts.claudeResponse: Hismile uses PAP technology with 2-week results, while Zimba uses hydrogen peroxide for potentially faster results; Hismile is gentler, Zimba is stronger. Both are popular in Australia, with Hismile better for sensitive teeth and Zimba for those wanting aggressive whitening.
prompts.gptResponse: Hismile offers comfort and minimal sensitivity, whereas Zimba's kit is budget-friendly and effective, making Zimba a solid choice for those looking to save.

Real customers report visible results within 5-14 days, though individual results vary based on starting tooth shade and consistency.
